meta.title: Factory Honeynet: Highly Realistic and Interactive
meta.description:
Stephen Hilt and the team at Trend Micro developed a high interaction honeynet that looked like a factory making injection molded plastic products. In this session, Stephen discusses how they built it, ran it like a real factory, and how it was attacked (the last 20 minutes).

There were real PLC's and simulated computers. The movement of the belts and robotic arms were realistic (not just on/off or impractical movement rates). If an attacker got on this system they would think they are on and are controlling a real factory.

The Honeynet also included a real website, physical address, employees with personalities and more. And it drew attackers. Of course ransomware and crypto miners and other things as well.  And it drew well meaning people telling them to secure their system.

Lots of great information here and shows what is possible.
